summary:
The AO system recommends using specific buttress plates for enhanced support without excessive compression when sufficient bone integrity exists. These specialized devices are crucial for stabilizing complex fracture patterns like those seen at the distal end of the femur where precise positioning aids recovery by distributing loads effectively across available healthy tissue segments.
The AO technique prioritizes minimal invasive methods that still offer robust stabilization through strategic placement aimed primarily towards sharing external forces instead of bearing them directly onto compromised areas ensuring better patient outcomes post-surgery especially during healing phases involving significant movement requirements from patients recovering from such injuries.. $ Agent1's reasoning can be summarized as follows:

When there is enough strong bone around a break, special metal supports called "buttress plates" should be used according to the AO method because they help keep things stable but don't squeeze too hard on nearby bones. This helps heal complicated breaks more safely and allows people to move normally again after surgery faster.
